articles.title: Europe Heads Into Winter With Its Weakest Gas Cushion In 15 Years
articles.description: Europe is unlikely to refill its natural gas storage facilities to even 80% before winter, Equinor CEO Anders Opedal said on Wednesday, Reuters reported, raising the prospect that the continent will enter the heating season with its smallest supply buffer in years. articles.title: Eni Côte d’Ivoire selects Saipem for $260m offshore drilling project
articles.description: Italian oilfield services company Saipem has secured a new offshore drilling contract valued at around $260m from Eni Côte d’Ivoire.

articles.title: The Hormuz Crisis Is Driving a Geopolitical Shift Across Asia
articles.description: For years, policymakers in Washington have viewed Asia primarily through the lens of Taiwan, the South China Sea, and China's military rise. articles.title: Iran War Escalation Threatens Global Fuel Supply Recovery
articles.description: Asian refiners that had bet on a flood of crude supply from the Middle East in August are now faced with potential delays in deliveries amid the re-escalation of hostilities, which could thwart their plans to ramp up crude processing rates in the coming weeks, analysts have told

articles.title: Pakistan Scrambles for Oil Alternatives as Hormuz, Red Sea Risks Mount
articles.description: Pakistan’s refiners are inquiring traders about potential crude oil supply from the U.S., Nigeria, Singapore, and central Asia, amid the escalating crisis in the Middle East that threatens supply from both the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea. articles.title: Oil Jumps Nearly 4% as Houthis Threaten Red Sea Blockade
articles.description: Oil prices jumped by nearly 4% early on Wednesday as concerns about crude supply from the Middle East intensify with threats to shipping at both chokepoints in the region, the Strait of Hormuz and the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. articles.title: GE Vernova wins order for six gas turbines to aid Hawaiian grid stability
articles.description: GE Vernova has secured a contract with Hawaiian Electric to supply six LM6000VELOX aeroderivative turbine packages for the Waiau Power Plant in O‘ahu.

articles.title: Japan’s Power Prices Soar to 3.5-Year High
articles.description: Japan’s day-ahead spot electricity prices surged on Wednesday to the highest level in three and a half years as soaring fuel costs, heat waves, and a weakening Japanese currency have combined to push power prices higher this summer. articles.title: Fluor selected for FEED of GPIC’s aromatics project in Bahrain
articles.description: Gulf Petrochemical Industries Company (GPIC) has appointed Fluor to oversee the front-end engineering and design (FEED) for a planned aromatics facility in Bahrain.

articles.title: TGS and Ghana Petroleum Commission sign Keta Basin seismic deal
articles.description: Energy data and intelligence provider TGS has signed an agreement with the Petroleum Commission, Ghana, to deliver a new offshore 2D-cubed seismic project in the Keta Basin, situated off the Ghanaian coast.

articles.title: Equinor Profit Soars 93% as Oil and Gas Price Spike Fuels Windfall
articles.description: Equinor (EQNR:NYSE) saw a 93% jump in its second-quarter profit from a year earlier as oil and gas prices soared during the Middle East crisis and delivered windfall earnings to the biggest energy firms. articles.title: China's Oil Output Hits Record High
articles.description: China produced a record amount of crude oil last year, at 216 million tons, with natural gas production also rising substantially, the National Energy Administration reported today. Together, the oil and gas output of China in terms of oil equivalent rose to 420 million tons—a re

articles.title: Surging Oil Prices Push Japan’s Import Bill to an Unprecedented High
articles.description: Surging oil prices have led to a significant increase in Japan’s import bill, hitting an all-time high last month of $89.46 billion, Reuters has reported, citing government data. The June total was 25.4% higher than a year earlier, as international oil prices rose by over 50% in

articles.title: Vår Energi to acquire BlueNord in $1.3bn cash-and-stock deal
articles.description: Vår Energi has agreed to acquire rival Norwegian oil and gas company BlueNord in a cash and stock deal worth Nkr12.84bn ($1.33bn).

articles.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Threatens Long-Term Energy Shock Across Asia

articles.title: Oil Prices Climb as U.S.-Iran Conflict Shows No Signs of Slowing
articles.description: Oil prices continued to climb in early Asian trade on Wednesday as the renewed hostilities between the United States and Iran showed no signs of slowing. articles.title: China's Next Move Could Decide Where Oil Prices Go This Year
articles.description: China’s demand for crude oil imports and the pace of its refined products exports will help shape the trend in oil prices through the end of the year, alongside the supply disruptions in the Middle East. articles.title: Fossil Fuels Still Generate 57% of the World's Electricity
articles.description: When the Energy Institute reported in its Statistical Review of World Energy that the global grid is still ruled by hydrocarbons, it may have come as a surprise to some observers. articles.title: US Crude Oil Inventories Build As Hormuz Shipping Headache Drags On
articles.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States rose by 2.603 million barrels in the week ending July 17. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories fell by 564,000 barrels. Although commercial crude oil inventories excluding the

articles.title: Energy IPOs Are Booming Thanks to AI’s Insatiable Thirst for Power
articles.description: The artificial intelligence boom has created an era of unprecedented uncertainty in the global economy. articles.title: ADNOC approves $6.2bn Umm Shaif gas cap development
articles.description: ADNOC has confirmed a Dh22.6bn ($6.2bn) final investment decision (FID) to develop the Umm Shaif Gas Cap in Abu Dhabi, UAE.

articles.title: Ansaldo returns to US market with Texas gas turbine order
articles.description: Ansaldo Energia has secured a contract to supply eight gas turbines for a Texas data centre power project, marking its return to US new-build generation.

articles.title: OMV Petrom completes Neptun Deep platform installation in Black Sea
articles.description: OMV Petrom, in partnership with S.N.G.N. ROMGAZ, has completed installation of the offshore production platform for the Neptun Deep gas project in the Black Sea.

articles.title: Halliburton wins Basra Oil contract for southern Iraq field services
articles.description: Halliburton has secured a contract from Basra Oil Company (BOC) to deliver Integrated Field Management Services (IFMS) for the development of the Bin Umar and Sindbad oil and gas fields located in southern Iraq.

articles.title: Magnolia to acquire WildFire Energy in $4.1bn Eagle Ford deal
articles.description: Magnolia Oil & Gas has agreed to acquire WildFire Energy, an oil and gas producer in East Texas’s Eagle Ford Shale, in a transaction valued at approximately $4.06bn, including outstanding debt.
